title: Fix 'in' operator crash on trip fields in driver frontend components

requirements.text: Scan DriverDashboard, DriverTripList, AvailableTripsSection, all TripCard components, and every helper/utility function in the frontend for any use of the JavaScript 'in' operator applied to trip fields (status, tripType, journeyType, vehicleType). Remove every such check and replace with safe comparison logic: if the value is a string, compare using ===; if the value is an object, extract the key using Object.keys(value)[0] before comparing.

quotes: Cannot use 'in' operator to search for '#local' in local
Remove ALL in operator checks on trip fields.
Use safe comparison logic: If typeof value === 'string' → compare using === If typeof value === 'object' → use Object.keys(value)[0]

acceptanceCriteria: No use of the 'in' operator on trip.status, trip.tripType, trip.journeyType, or trip.vehicleType exists anywhere in DriverDashboard, DriverTripList, AvailableTripsSection, TripCard components, or any helper/utility files.
All comparisons on those fields use === (for strings) or Object.keys(value)[0] (for objects).
The driver dashboard no longer throws 'Cannot use in operator to search for #local in local' after login.

requirements.text: Ensure every trip data source rendered inside DriverDashboard, DriverTripList, and AvailableTripsSection is passed through the existing normalizeTrip() utility before being used in render logic or passed to child components, so all trip fields are guaranteed plain strings at render time.

quotes: Also ensure all trip sources are normalized through normalizeTrip() before rendering.

acceptanceCriteria: All trip arrays or trip objects in DriverDashboard, DriverTripList, and AvailableTripsSection are mapped through normalizeTrip() before any field access or rendering.
No raw backend trip objects reach TripCard or any rendering component without normalization.
Driver dashboard loads without runtime errors after login.

constraints: No scope changes beyond fixing the 'in' operator crash and ensuring normalizeTrip() is applied — do not add, remove, or alter any other features or UI.
nonGoals: Changes to backend data models or Motoko code.
Adding new trip fields or normalizing fields beyond status, tripType, journeyType, and vehicleType.
Modifying any file listed under frontend.immutablePaths.
